title: rubus guyanensis
intro: Rubus guyanensis, also known as the Guianan raspberry or wild raspberry, is a species of raspberry native to South America, characterized by its thorny canes and edible fruit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rubus guyanensis include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce the risk of heart disease, and provide antioxidant protection against cell damage. Herbal preparations of Rubus guyanensis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support cardiovascular health and immune function.
